gapClosures.sourceRisk: provider events can be consumed directly by business logic
gapClosures.resolution: All webhook and polling inputs are converted into TelephonyProviderEvent canonical events before CallSession or readiness logic can run.

gapClosures.sourceRisk: recording landed means the phone path is ready
gapClosures.resolution: Recording availability, transcript readiness, evidence readiness, and continuation eligibility are separate records with separate truth-table rows.

gapClosures.sourceRisk: seeded continuation can be chosen from identity confidence alone
gapClosures.resolution: TelephonyContinuationEligibility must cite the latest evidence readiness assessment plus identity and destination confidence references before any grant recommendation.

gapClosures.sourceRisk: manual audio review is just an operator convention
gapClosures.resolution: TelephonyManualReviewDisposition is a first-class closure-blocking contract for recording-missing, transcript-degraded, contradictory-capture, identity-ambiguous, handset-untrusted, and urgent-live-without-routine-evidence cases.

gapClosures.sourceRisk: patient continuation UI can promise more than the current evidence posture supports
gapClosures.resolution: The mobile continuation grammar is bounded to seeded, challenge, urgent-live-only, manual-review-only, and recovery postures backed by the same readiness truth table.

gapClosures.sourceRisk: out-of-order or duplicated webhooks can be hand-waved away by the provider SDK
gapClosures.resolution: CallSession rebuild is defined from canonical telephony events plus immutable readiness records using idempotency keys and monotonic sequence handling.
